title: Messaging Outcomes, Delivery Claims, and Evidence · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter for messaging outcomes, delivery claims, and evidence. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome.
Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics.
State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 8143 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate local publish, broker ownership, and observable stakeholder effect; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome.
